extra.reasoning:
The single output acuity is ordered self_care < routine < urgent < emergent. Conflict resolution picks the highest-priority matching rule. palpitations appears only in r3 (priority 100, the dominating priority), which forces acuity := self_care (the lowest level). Setting palpitations true can therefore ENABLE r3 and drag acuity down.

Counterexample (witness is the palpitations=true state):
Base input: pallor=T, vision_loss=T, fever=T, sweating=T, palpitations=FALSE, focal_weakness=F.
  Firing rules: r1 (sweating, p3 -> emergent), r6 (vision_loss, p3 -> emergent), r4 (fever, p2 -> urgent), r5 (pallor, p2 -> urgent). r3 does NOT fire (palpitations false). Highest priority is 3 -> emergent. Result = emergent.

Flip only palpitations to TRUE: pallor=T, vision_loss=T, fever=T, sweating=T, palpitations=TRUE, focal_weakness=F.
  Now r3 fires (all five conjuncts true) at priority 100, which dominates every other rule. Result = self_care.

Thus setting palpitations true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, violating the monotonicity property (palpitations true must never lower acuity).
